description:
You are an observability engineer tuning a dashboard for a
    payments platform.  To verify that the alert configuration is
    catching every production incident, you need a clean sample of all
    log entries that

        • were written on 2023-07-20 (UTC)  
        • are emitted at the ERROR level

    A synthetic application log has already been placed in the
    workspace for you:

        /home/user/observability/input/app.log

    1. Read only that file; do not alter or move it.  
    2. Using **regular-expression‐based filtering** (e.g., grep ‑E or
       sed with a proper pattern), extract every line that satisfies
       the two bullet conditions above, preserving the original order.
    3. Create an output directory

        /home/user/observability/output

       if it does not yet exist, then write the filtered lines into

        /home/user/observability/output/2023-07-20_error.log

    4. Create a companion file that contains nothing except the number
       of extracted lines followed by a single newline:

        /home/user/observability/output/2023-07-20_error.count

       • No extra spaces, words, or blank lines are allowed—only an
         ASCII decimal integer and a trailing newline ( “\n” ).  
       • The automated test will read this file **byte-for-byte**.

    Hints (not requirements):
       – A concise pipeline of 3-4 commands (mkdir, grep, tee, wc)
         is sufficient.  
       – A robust regex anchor such as “^2023-07-20.*ERROR” will match
         the intended lines.

    Success criteria checked by the grader:
       • /home/user/observability/output/2023-07-20_error.log exists,
         is non-empty, and contains *only* the correct ERROR lines from
         2023-07-20, in the same order as in the source log.  
       • /home/user/observability/output/2023-07-20_error.count exists
         and contains the exact decimal line count with one trailing
         newline.  
       • The original app.log remains unchanged.

    You have regular user privileges; no sudo is available.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-populated data present when the scenario starts
    -----------------------------------------------
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/observability/input/app.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-19 10:13:45,123 INFO  PaymentsService - Starting reconciliation
2023-07-19 10:13:45,456 ERROR PaymentsService - Failed to reconcile invoice 8745
2023-07-19 11:07:12,789 WARN  AuthService - Token expired for user 102
2023-07-19 11:07:15,001 INFO  AuthService - Token refreshed for user 102
2023-07-20 09:03:05,321 ERROR OrdersService - Order 5561 could not be processed
2023-07-20 09:03:06,555 INFO  OrdersService - Retry queued for order 5561
2023-07-20 12:47:17,678 ERROR InventoryService - Stock level mismatch for SKU 887
2023-07-20 12:47:18,999 WARN  InventoryService - Retrying stock update
2023-07-20 20:00:00,555 ERROR AuthService - Authentication DB unreachable
2023-07-21 08:22:55,111 INFO  PaymentsService - Reconciliation complete
2023-07-21 08:23:00,222 ERROR PaymentsService - Payment gateway timeout
2023-07-21 14:15:42,333 INFO  OrdersService - Order 5561 processed successfully
2023-07-21 14:16:42,444 ERROR OrdersService - Refund failed for order 5561
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <!--
    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
    -----------------------------------------------
    -->
    <expected_output_files>
        <file path="/home/user/observability/output/2023-07-20_error.log"><![CDATA[
2023-07-20 09:03:05,321 ERROR OrdersService - Order 5561 could not be processed
2023-07-20 12:47:17,678 ERROR InventoryService - Stock level mismatch for SKU 887
2023-07-20 20:00:00,555 ERROR AuthService - Authentication DB unreachable
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/observability/output/2023-07-20_error.count"><![CDATA[
3
]]></file>
    </expected_output_files>
